signals.title: USD/GBP — G8 Bilateral Theme + UK Political Crisis
signals.text: USD/GBP spread at 2.019% marks a dominant G8 theme as the British Pound dives to five-week lows below 1.3350 amid a deepening UK political crisis. USD outperformance (+1.076% avg) is fueled by soaring US yields and Fed hike bets, while GBP (-0.943% avg) faces aggressive liquidation. Despite a neutral 0bp carry spread (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%), the political risk premium is driving a structural break in GBP/USD. Watch for a sustained break of 1.3300 to accelerate the move.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Fed Hike Bets
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (long% at 18.5% extreme) vs RBNZ hike bias with a next meeting on 27 May. While specs are heavily short, the pair has tumbled to 0.5844 on renewed Fed rate hike bets and DXY strength above 99.00. This creates a high-conviction short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ delivers a hawkish surprise, as speculative positioning is at a historical extreme against the central bank's hiking stance. NZD/USD testing 0.5840 support.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Options Skew + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.52 shows a strong 25d RR put skew of -1.69, signaling the options market is paying a significant premium for downside protection despite the +286bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.59% vs TONA 0.73%). This bearish options bias aligns with a correlation break (z=-1.84σ) where JPY is failing to track US 10Y yields. The divergence suggests institutional hedging against BoJ intervention risk as price holds near the 160.00 critical threshold. Watch for BoJ verbal warnings to trigger a volatility spike.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Elevated Volatility Risk Premium
signals.text: GBP/USD IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 7.8% generates a +3.2% Vol Risk Premium (VRP), indicating the options market is pricing in significantly higher future volatility than has been realized over the last 30 days. This elevated premium reflects intense hedging demand surrounding the UK political crisis and upcoming BoE meeting on 18 Jun. Negative 25d RR at -0.88 confirms the bias is for downside protection. Expect wide intraday ranges while VRP remains above 2.0%.

signals.title: GBP/CHF — Retail Sentiment Extreme
signals.text: GBP/CHF retail sentiment at 90% long vs 10% short (Myfxbook) represents an extreme contrarian signal. Retail traders are aggressively fading the GBP weakness driven by the UK political crisis, while institutional COT data for GBP remains net long (+31,244). This 90% retail long extreme typically precedes further downside as retail positions are forced into liquidation. GBP/CHF at 1.0486; watch for a break of 1.0450 to trigger retail capitulation.
